_description: A middle-income borrower who negotiates hard but eventually accepts. Steady job, some savings. Pushes back on every term, asks for discounts, but ultimately commits to the installment plan.
AssessmentAgent: Yes, I received your notice. What is this about exactly?
My monthly income is around 90000 but it varies.
Salaried, I work in manufacturing, 8 years tenure.
Assets total about 4500000 — mostly a flat plus some savings.
Other liabilities: home loan 2500000 and car loan 150000 outstanding.
Cash flow is tight this year because of medical bills.
Anything else you need from me?
ResolutionAgent: What are my options here? I want to resolve this.
The down payment is too high for me. Can we cut it in half?
Look, 7225 a month is manageable but 12750 upfront is a lot. Can you spread the down payment over 2 months?
Fine, if those are the rules. I commit to the installment plan as stated.
FinalNoticeAgent: I hear you. Just confirm the first installment date and I will pay.
Yes, I formally accept the terms you outlined.
